Use context7 and go through literally all

This are the pages:

Landing & marketing
/ — Homepage
/how-it-works — How it works

Auth

Onboarding

/onboarding/profile — Profile
/onboarding/preferences — Preferences

Main app
/home — Dashboard
/library — Library
/create — Create

Sanctuary

/sanctuary/credits — Credits

Affirmations

/sanctuary/affirmations/record — Record
Rituals

/sanctuary/rituals/[id]/edit — Edit
/sanctuary/rituals/create — Create (and steps: init, values, strengths, goals, patterns, tone, language, script, review, record, enhance, complete)
/sanctuary/rituals/recordings — Recordings
Useful links
/showcase — Design system & UI components
/pages — Index of all pages

        "contents": "import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';\n\n/**\n * Proxy for protected routes\n * Checks authentication state and redirects if needed\n *\n * Note: This is a basic implementation. For production, you may want to\n * verify the session server-side or use a more robust approach.\n */\nexport function proxy(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  // Public routes that don't require authentication\n  const publicRoutes = ['/', '/login', '/signup', '/forgot-password', '/reset-password', '/how-it-works', '/pricing'];\n\n  // Check if route is public\n  const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.some(route =&gt; pathname === route || pathname.startsWith(route + '/'));\n\n  // Protected routes (main app routes)\n  const isProtectedRoute = pathname.startsWith('/home') ||\n                           pathname.startsWith('/library') ||\n                           pathname.startsWith('/create') ||\n                           pathname.startsWith('/profile');\n\n  // For protected routes, we'll handle redirect in the layout component\n  // since we need client-side auth state check\n  // Proxy can't access client-side state easily\n\n  return NextResponse.next();\n}\n\nexport const config = {\n  matcher: [\n    /*\n     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:\n     * - api (API routes)\n     * - _next/static (static files)\n     * - _next/image (image optimization files)\n     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)\n     * - public files (public folder)\n     */\n,\n};",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\nimport { useForm, Controller } from 'react-hook-form';\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';\nimport { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { Container, Typography, Button, Input } from '@/components';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/theme';\nimport { AnimatedBackground, Logo } from '@/components';\nimport { spacing, borderRadius } from '@/theme';\nimport { signupSchema } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport { Mail, Lock, ArrowRight, Eye, EyeOff, CheckCircle2 } from 'lucide-react';\nimport type { SignupFormData } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\n\nexport default function SignupPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { signup, isLoading, error, setError, resendVerificationEmail } = useAuthStore();\n  const [signupSuccess, setSignupSuccess] = useState(false);\n  const [userEmail, setUserEmail] = useState('');\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [showConfirmPassword, setShowConfirmPassword] = useState(false);\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n    watch,\n  } = useForm&lt;SignupFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(signupSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n      password: '',\n      confirmPassword: '',\n      acceptTerms: false,\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: SignupFormData) =&gt; {\n    setError(null);\n    const result = await signup(data.email, data.password);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      setUserEmail(data.email);\n      setSignupSuccess(true);\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  const handleResendVerification = async () =&gt; {\n    if (userEmail) {\n      await resendVerificationEmail(userEmail);\n    }\n  };\n\n  if (signupSuccess) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/web/src/components/auth/AuthProvider.tsx",
        "range": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 66,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n\n [initializeAuth]);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Handle protected route redirects\n    if (!isReady || !isInitialized) return;\n\n    const publicRoutes = ['/', '/login', '/signup', '/forgot-password', '/reset-password', '/how-it-works', '/pricing'];\n    const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.includes(pathname) || pathname.startsWith('/showcase');\n    const isProtectedRoute = pathname.startsWith('/home') || \n                            pathname.startsWith('/library') || \n                            pathname.startsWith('/create') || \n                            pathname.startsWith('/profile');\n\n    if (isProtectedRoute &amp;&amp; !user) {\n      // Redirect to login if trying to access protected route without auth\n      router.push('/login');\n    } else if (!isPublicRoute &amp;&amp; !isProtectedRoute &amp;&amp; pathname !== '/') {\n      // If on an unknown route and not authenticated, redirect to home or login\n      if (!user) {\n        router.push('/login');\n      }\n    }\n  }, [user, isReady, isInitialized, pathname, router]);\n\n  // Show nothing while initializing\n  if (!isReady || !isInitialized) {\n    return null; // Or a loading screen component\n  }\n\n  return &lt;&gt;{children}&lt;/&gt;;\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/web/app/api/auth/override/route.ts",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 43
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 36,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "/**\n * Override login: validate credentials against env and allow bypassing Supabase.\n * Use only for dev/admin access. Set OVERRIDE_LOGIN_EMAIL and OVERRIDE_LOGIN_PASSWORD in env.\n */\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  try {\n    const body = await request.json();\n    const email = typeof body?.email === 'string' ? body.email.trim() : '';\n    const password = typeof body?.password === 'string' ? body.password : '';\n\n    const envEmail = process.env.OVERRIDE_LOGIN_EMAIL?.trim();\n    const envPassword = process.env.OVERRIDE_LOGIN_PASSWORD;\n\n    if (!envEmail || !envPassword) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: 'Override login not configured' },\n        { status: 501 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (email === envEmail &amp;&amp; password === envPassword) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    }\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: 'Invalid credentials' },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: 'Bad request' },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "/**",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": " * Override login: validate credentials against env and allow bypassing Supabase.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": " * Use only for dev/admin access. Set OVERRIDE_LOGIN_EMAIL and OVERRIDE_LOGIN_PASSWORD in env.",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": " */",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "export async function POST(request: Request) {",
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  try {",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "    const body = await request.json();",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\nimport { useRouter, usePathname } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport { getOverrideUserToRestore } from '@/lib/auth-override';\n\n/**\n * Auth Provider Component\n * Initializes auth state and handles protected route redirects\n */\nexport function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n  const { user, isInitialized, initializeAuth } = useAuthStore();\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const pathname = usePathname();\n  const [isReady, setIsReady] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Initialize auth state on mount\n    let unsubscribe: (() =&gt; void) | null = null;\n    \n    initializeAuth().then((unsub) =&gt; {\n      unsubscribe = unsub;\n      // If no Supabase session but override is stored, restore override user so pages are visible\n      const overrideUser = getOverrideUserToRestore();\n      if (overrideUser) {\n        useAuthStore.getState().setUser(overrideUser);\n      }\n      setIsReady(true);\n    });\n    \n    return () =&gt; {\n      if (unsubscribe) {\n        unsubscribe();\n      }\n    };\n  }, [initializeAuth]);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Handle protected route redirects\n    if (!isReady || !isInitialized) return;\n  }\n}",

        "contents": "export default function ResetPasswordPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { resetPassword, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const authService = createAuthService(supabase);\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [showConfirmPassword, setShowConfirmPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [hasValidSession, setHasValidSession] = useState(false);\n  \n  // Check for Supabase session from hash fragments (handled automatically by Supabase)\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    const checkSession = async () =&gt; {\n      // Supabase automatically processes hash fragments and creates a session\n      // We just need to check if we have a session\n      const sessionResult = await authService.getCurrentSession();\n      setHasValidSession(!!sessionResult.data);\n    };\n    \n    checkSession();\n  }, [authService]);\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;ResetPasswordFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(resetPasswordSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      password: '',\n      confirmPassword: '',\n      token: '', // Token handled by Supabase from URL hash\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: ResetPasswordFormData) =&gt; {\n    if (!hasValidSession) {\n      setError('Invalid or expired reset link. Please request a new password reset.');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    setError(null);\n    // Supabase handles token from URL hash fragments automatically\n    const result = await resetPassword(data.password);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      router.push('/login?message=Password reset successful. Please sign in with your new password.');\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  if (!hasValidSession) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div\n}",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\nimport { useForm, Controller } from 'react-hook-form';\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';\nimport { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { Container, Typography, Button, Input } from '@/components';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/theme';\nimport { AnimatedBackground, Logo } from '@/components';\nimport { spacing, borderRadius } from '@/theme';\nimport { forgotPasswordSchema } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport { Mail, ArrowRight, CheckCircle2 } from 'lucide-react';\nimport type { ForgotPasswordFormData } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\n\nexport default function ForgotPasswordPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { requestPasswordReset, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const [emailSent, setEmailSent] = useState(false);\n  const [userEmail, setUserEmail] = useState('');\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;ForgotPasswordFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(forgotPasswordSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: ForgotPasswordFormData) =&gt; {\n    setError(null);\n    // For web, use the reset password page URL\n    const redirectTo = &#96;${window.location.origin}/reset-password&#96;;\n    const result = await requestPasswordReset(data.email, redirectTo);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      setUserEmail(data.email);\n      setEmailSent(true);\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  if (emailSent) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div\n}",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { useForm, Controller } from 'react-hook-form';\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';\nimport { useRouter, useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { Container, Typography, Button, Input } from '@/components';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/theme';\nimport { AnimatedBackground, Logo } from '@/components';\nimport { spacing, borderRadius } from '@/theme';\nimport { loginSchema } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport { applyOverrideLogin } from '@/lib/auth-override';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport { Mail, Lock, ArrowRight, Eye, EyeOff } from 'lucide-react';\nimport type { LoginFormData } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\n\nexport default function LoginPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams();\n  const { login, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = React.useState(false);\n  const message = searchParams.get('message');\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;LoginFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(loginSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n      password: '',\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: LoginFormData) =&gt;\n;\n}",

        "contents": "export default function SignupPage() {\nconst \n;\n\n  if (signupSuccess) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div style={{ minHeight: '100vh', position: 'relative', overflow: 'hidden' }}&gt;\n        &lt;AnimatedBackground intensity=\"medium\" color=\"primary\" /&gt;\n        \n        &lt;div\n          style={{\n            position: 'fixed',\n            inset: 0,\n            background: colors.gradients.mystical,\n            pointerEvents: 'none',\n            zIndex: 0,\n          }}\n        /&gt;\n\n        &lt;div style={{ position: 'relative', zIndex: 1, minHeight: '100vh', display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center', padding: spacing.xl }}&gt;\n          &lt;div style={{ width: '100%', maxWidth: '480px' }}&gt;\n            &lt;div\n              style={{\n                padding: spacing.xxxl,\n                borderRadius: borderRadius.xl,\n                background: colors.glass.opaque,\n                backdropFilter: 'blur(20px)',\n                WebkitBackdropFilter: 'blur(20px)',\n                border: &#96;1px solid ${colors.glass.border}&#96;,\n                boxShadow: &#96;0 16px 64px ${colors.mystical.glow}40&#96;,\n                textAlign: 'center',\n              }}\n            &gt;\n              &lt;CheckCircle2 size={64} color={colors.success} style={{ margin: '0 auto', marginBottom: spacing.lg }} /&gt;\n              &lt;Typography variant=\"h2\" style={{ color: colors.text.primary, marginBottom: spacing.md }}&gt;\n                Check Your Email\n              &lt;/Typography&gt;\n              &lt;Typography variant=\"body\" style={{ color: colors.text.secondary, marginBottom: spacing.xl, lineHeight: '24px' }}&gt;\n                We've sent a verification email to &lt;strong&gt;{userEmail}&lt;/strong&gt;. Please check your inbox and click the verification link to activate your account.\n              &lt;/Typography&gt;\n              &lt;Button\n                variant=\"primary\"\n}",

        "contents": "const CONTENT_TYPES: ContentType[] = [\n  {\n    name: 'Create Affirmation',\n    description: 'Powerful statements to rewire your subconscious mind',\n    icon: Sparkles,\n    href: '/affirmations/create',\n    color: 'primary',\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'Create Meditation',\n    description: 'Guided meditations for deep relaxation and transformation',\n    icon: Brain,\n    href: '/meditations/create',\n    color: 'secondary',\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'Create Ritual',\n    description: 'Personalized rituals combining voice, frequencies, and intention',\n    icon: Music,\n    href: '/rituals/create',\n    color: 'tertiary',\n  },\n];\n\nexport default function CreatePage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n\n  return (\n    &lt;Container&gt;\n      &lt;ThemeSelector /&gt;\n      &lt;AnimatedBackground intensity=\"medium\" color=\"primary\" /&gt;\n      \n      &lt;div\n        style={{\n          minHeight: '100vh',\n}",

        "contents": "export function proxy(request: NextRequest) {\n\n}\n\nexport const config = {\n  matcher: [\n    /*\n     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:\n     * - api (API routes)\n     * - _next/static (static files)\n     * - _next/image (image optimization files)\n     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)\n     * - public files (public folder)\n     */\n    '/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',\n  ],\n};",
        "signatures": {},

            "lineNumber": 32

            "lineNumber": 33

            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    /*",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },

            "text": "     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },

            "text": "     * - api (API routes)",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 42

          {

            "lineNumber": 44
          },

        "contents": "export default function LoginPage() {\n\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: LoginFormData) =&gt; {\n    setError(null);\n    const result = await login(data.email, data.password);\n\n    if (result.success) {\n      router.push('/home');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Try override login (env-configured admin/dev credentials)\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch('/api/auth/override', {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ email: data.email, password: data.password }),\n      });\n      const json = res.ok ? await res.json() : null;\n      if (json?.ok) {\n        const overrideUser = applyOverrideLogin(data.email);\n        useAuthStore.getState().setUser(overrideUser);\n        useAuthStore.getState().setSession(null);\n        useAuthStore.getState().setError(null);\n        router.push('/home');\n        return;\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // fall through to existing error\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store from Supabase login\n  };\n\n  return (\n    &lt;div style={{ minHeight: '100vh', position: 'relative',\n}",

        "contents": "export default function SignupPage() {\n\n\n              &lt;Button\n                type=\"submit\"\n                variant=\"primary\"\n                size=\"lg\"\n                loading={isLoading}\n                fullWidth\n                style={{\n                  background: colors.gradients.primary,\n                  marginBottom: spacing.lg,\n                  height: '52px',\n                  fontSize: '16px',\n                  fontWeight: 600,\n                }}\n              &gt;\n                {isLoading ? 'Creating account...' : 'Create Account'}\n              &lt;/Button&gt;\n\n              &lt;div style={{ textAlign: 'center', marginTop: spacing.lg }}&gt;\n                &lt;Typography variant=\"body\" style={{ color: colors.text.secondary }}&gt;\n                  Already have an account?{' '}\n                  &lt;Link\n                    href=\"/login\"\n                    style={{\n                      color: colors.accent.primary,\n                      textDecoration: 'none',\n                      fontWeight: 600,\n                    }}\n                  &gt;\n                    Sign in\n                  &lt;/Link&gt;\n                &lt;/Typography&gt;\n              &lt;/div&gt;\n            &lt;/form&gt;\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n\n          {/* Back to Home */}\n          &lt;div style={{ textAlign: 'center', marginTop: spacing.xl }}&gt;\n            &lt;Link\n              href=\"/\"\n              style={{\n                display: 'inline-flex',\n                alignItems: 'center',\n                gap: spacing.sm,\n                color: colors.text.secondary,\n                textDecoration: 'none',\n                fontSize: '14px',\n              }}\n            &gt;\n              &lt;ArrowRight size={16} color={colors.text.secondary} style={{ transform: 'rotate(180deg)' }} /&gt;\n              Back to home\n            &lt;/Link&gt;\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n        &lt;/div&gt;\n      &lt;/div&gt;\n    &lt;/div&gt;\n  );\n}",

{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Define a Root Layout in Next.js App Directory\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/02-guides/migrating/app-router-migration.mdx\\n\\nThis code defines a &#96;RootLayout&#96; component, typically located at &#96;app/layout.tsx&#96; or &#96;app/layout.js&#96;. It serves as the top-level UI that wraps all routes within the &#96;app&#96; directory, accepting &#96;children&#96; to render nested layouts or pages. A root layout is mandatory for the &#96;app&#96; directory and must include &#96;&lt;html&gt;&#96; and &#96;&lt;body&gt;&#96; tags.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\nexport default function RootLayout({\\n  // Layouts must accept a children prop.\\n  // This will be populated with nested layouts or pages\\n  children,\\n}: {\\n  children: React.ReactNode\\n}) {\\n  return (\\n    &lt;html lang=\\\"en\\\"&gt;\\n      &lt;body&gt;{children}&lt;/body&gt;\\n    &lt;/html&gt;\\n  )\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;jsx\\nexport default function RootLayout({\\n  // Layouts must accept a children prop.\\n  // This will be populated with nested layouts or pages\\n  children,\\n}) {\\n  return (\\n    &lt;html lang=\\\"en\\\"&gt;\\n      &lt;body&gt;{children}&lt;/body&gt;\\n    &lt;/html&gt;\\n  )\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Define Root Layout in Next.js App Router\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/01-installation.mdx\\n\\nThis code defines the root layout for a Next.js application using the App Router. It is a required component that wraps all other pages and must contain the &#96;&lt;html&gt;&#96; and &#96;&lt;body&gt;&#96; tags, receiving &#96;children&#96; as its content.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\nexport default function RootLayout({\\n  children,\\n}: {\\n  children: React.ReactNode\\n}) {\\n  return (\\n    &lt;html lang=\\\"en\\\"&gt;\\n      &lt;body&gt;{children}&lt;/body&gt;\\n    &lt;/html&gt;\\n  )\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;jsx\\nexport default function RootLayout({ children }) {\\n  return (\\n    &lt;html lang=\\\"en\\\"&gt;\\n      &lt;body&gt;{children}&lt;/body&gt;\\n    &lt;/html&gt;\\n  )\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Implement Next.js nested layout for blog section\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/03-layouts-and-pages.mdx\\n\\nThis snippet shows how to define a nested layout specifically for the &#96;/blog&#96; route and its children. By adding a &#96;layout&#96; file inside the &#96;app/blog&#96; folder, this layout will wrap all pages within the blog segment, providing a consistent UI structure.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\nexport default function BlogLayout({\\n  children,\\n}: {\\n  children: React.ReactNode\\n}) {\\n  return &lt;section&gt;{children}&lt;/section&gt;\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;jsx\\nexport default function BlogLayout({ children }) {\\n  return &lt;section&gt;{children}&lt;/section&gt;\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### R &gt; Route Groups\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/04-glossary.mdx\\n\\nA way to organize routes without affecting the URL structure. Created by wrapping a folder name in parentheses (e.g., &#96;(marketing)&#96;), route groups help organize related routes and enable per-group [layouts](#layout). Learn more in [Route Groups](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route-groups).\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Route groups\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/02-project-structure.mdx\\n\\nRoute groups can be created by wrapping a folder in parenthesis: &#96;(folderName)&#96;. This indicates the folder is for organizational purposes and should **not be included** in the route's URL path. Route groups are useful for organizing routes by site section, intent, or team (such as marketing pages or admin pages), enabling nested layouts in the same route segment level, creating multiple nested layouts in the same segment including multiple root layouts, and adding a layout to a subset of routes in a common segment.\"}]}"
}

        "contents": "## Phase 5: Content Definitions &amp; Types\n\n### Step 5.1: Define Content Type System\n**Goal**: Implement the three content types with proper definitions\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create TypeScript types for Affirmation, Meditation, Ritual\n- [ ] Define structure for each type (based on scientific foundations)\n- [ ] Create content type enums and constants\n- [ ] Add validation schemas (using Zod)\n- [ ] Document differences between types\n- [ ] Create content factory/helper functions\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Type system for content\n- Validation schemas\n\n**Success Criteria**: Types are well-defined, validation works\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: No UI changes, but types are ready\n\n---\n\n### Step 5.2: Build Content Detail Screens (Empty)\n**Goal**: Create screens for viewing individual content items\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create Affirmation detail screen (structure only)\n- [ ] Create Meditation detail screen (structure only)\n- [ ] Create Ritual detail screen (structure only)\n- [ ] Add placeholder for audio player\n- [ ] Add placeholder for content text\n- [ ] Add action buttons (Play, Edit, Delete, Share)\n- [ ] Add metadata display (duration, created date, tags)\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Three detail screens with structure\n\n**Success Criteria**: Can navigate to detail screens, see structure\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See detail screens with placeholders for all elements\n\n---\n\n### Step 5.3: Build Content Creation Flows (Structure Only)\n**Goal**: Create multi-step creation flows for each content type\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create Affirmation creation flow (steps: Intent, Script, Voice, Review)\n- [ ] Create Meditation creation flow (steps: Intent, Context, Script, Voice, Review)\n- [ ] Create Ritual creation flow (steps: Intent, Context, Personalization, Script, Voice, Review)\n- [ ] Add step indicator component\n- [ ] Add navigation between steps\n- [ ] Add form inputs for each step (non-functional)\n- [ ] Add \"Next\" and \"Back\" buttons\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Three creation flows with all steps\n\n**Success Criteria**: Can navigate through all steps in each flow\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See multi-step creation flows, can navigate steps, see all form fields",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { Typography, Button } from '@/components';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/theme';\nimport { AnimatedBackground, Logo } from '@/components';\nimport { spacing, borderRadius } from '@/theme';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport {\n  Sunrise,\n  Bell,\n  Brain,\n  Zap,\n  Heart,\n  Music,\n  Clock,\n  Download,\n  Star,\n  Users,\n  Sparkles,\n  ArrowRight,\n  Check,\n} from 'lucide-react';\n\nconst steps = [\n  {\n    icon: Sunrise,\n    title: 'Morning Ritual',\n    description: 'Start your day with intention',\n    detail: 'Begin each morning with a personalized combination of meditation and powerful affirmations, designed to align with your energy and goals.',\n    highlight: 'AI-powered personalization adapts to your preferences',\n  },\n  {\n    icon: Bell,\n    title: 'Daily Reminders',\n    description: 'Stay consistent with gentle nudges',\n    detail: 'Receive thoughtful notifications at your preferred times, helping you maintain your practice and build lasting habits.',\n    highlight: 'Smart scheduling based on your most successful sessions',\n  },\n  {\n    icon: Brain,\n    title: 'Sacred Guide',\n    description: 'Your personal spiritual companion',\n    detail: 'Let our intelligent system guide your growth with personalized recommendations for meditations, affirmations, and practices.',\n    highlight: 'Learns and evolves with your spiritual journey',\n  },\n  {\n    icon: Zap,\n    title: 'Track Your Growth',\n    description: 'Visualize your transformation',\n    detail: 'Monitor your progress with beautiful insights, celebrate streaks, and unlock achievements as you deepen your practice.',\n    highlight: 'Watch your consistency build lasting change',\n  },\n];\n\nconst benefits = [\n  {\n    icon: Heart,\n    title: 'Personalized Experience',\n,\n];",

Implementation Steps\n\n### Step 5.1: Define Content Type System\n**Goal**: Implement the three content types with proper definitions\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create TypeScript types for Affirmation, Meditation, Ritual\n- [ ] Define structure for each type (based on scientific foundations)\n- [ ] Create content type enums and constants\n- [ ] Add validation schemas (using Zod)\n- [ ] Document differences between types\n- [ ] Create content factory/helper functions\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: No UI changes, but types are ready\n\n---\n\n### Step 5.2: Build Content Detail Screens (Empty)\n**Goal**: Create screens for viewing individual content items\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create Affirmation detail screen (structure only)\n- [ ] Create Meditation detail screen (structure only)\n- metadata display (duration, created date, tags)\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See detail screens with placeholders for all elements\n\n---\n\n### Step 5.3: Build Content Creation Flows (Structure navigation between steps\n- [ ] Add form inputs for each step (non-functional)\n- [ ] Add \"Next\" and \"Back\" buttons\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See multi-step creation flows, can navigate steps, see all form fields\n\n---\n\n## Verification Checklist\n\n### Code Quality",

None (structure only, no data yet)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Steps\n\n### Step 4.1: Build Home/Sanctuary Screen\n**Goal**: Create main dashboard screen with empty state\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design home screen layout\n- [ ] Add header with user greeting\n- [ ] Add empty state for no content\n- [ ] Add quick action buttons (Create Affirmation, Create Meditation, Create Ritual)\n- [ ] Add navigation to other sections\n- [ ] Add placeholder for stats\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See home screen with empty states and action buttons\n\n---\n\n### Step 4.2: Build Library Screen\n**Goal**: Create content library screen (empty)\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design library screen layout\n- [ ] Add filter tabs (All, Affirmations, Meditations, Rituals)\n- [ ] Add search bar (non-functional for now)\n- [ ] Add empty state with illustration\n- [ ] Add grid/list view toggle (placeholder)\n- [ ] Add sorting options (placeholder)\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See library screen with filters and empty state\n\n---\n\n### Step 4.3: Build Create Screen\n**Goal**: Create content creation entry point\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design create screen with three options\n- [ ] Add cards for: Create Affirmation, Create Meditation, Create Ritual\n- [ ] Add descriptions for each type\n- [ ] Add navigation to creation flows (empty flows for now)\n- [ ] Add visual indicators (icons, colors)\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See create screen with three beautiful option cards\n\n---\n\n### Step 4.4: Build Profile/Settings Screen\n**Goal**: Create user profile and settings screen\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design profile screen layout\n- [ ] Add user info section (name, email, avatar placeholder)\n- [ ] Add settings list (Preferences, Notifications, Credits, About, Logout)\n- [ ] Add navigation to each setting section\n- [ ] Add logout functionality\n- [ ] Add app version display\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See profile screen with settings list, logout works\n\n---\n\n## Verification Checklist\n\n### Code Quality",

        "contents": "3. **Are types shared?** → Import from shared\n4. **Is error handling proper?** → Add error handling\n5. **Are loading states handled?** → Add loading states\n6. **Is this production-ready?** → Review against standards\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n### ✅ DO\n- Put business logic in &#96;packages/shared/&#96;\n- Import types from shared\n- Use shared services for API calls\n- Use shared stores for state\n- Use shared schemas for validation\n- Follow consistent architecture\n- Test on all platforms\n- Update changelog\n\n### ❌ DON'T\n- Duplicate code across platforms\n- Create types in platform folders\n- Create API services in platform folders\n- Create stores in platform folders\n- Create validation schemas in platform folders\n- Skip error handling\n- Skip loading states\n- Skip empty states\n- Commit without testing\n\n---\n\n## Database &amp; Schema\n\n### Current Schema\n- **Same database as web app** - Supabase PostgreSQL\n- **Reference**: &#96;rebuild-roadmap/01-planning/02-schema-verification.md&#96;\n\n### Missing Tables (To Be Created)\n- &#96;content_items&#96; - Unified table for affirmations/meditations/rituals\n- &#96;credit_transactions&#96; - Credit system tracking\n- &#96;conversations&#96; - Conversational creation flow\n\n### Schema Changes\n- **Always verify schemas** - check coherence\n- **Create migrations** - for schema changes\n- **Update docs** - document schema changes\n- **Test RLS policies** - verify security\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Workflow\n\n### Before Starting a Step\n\n1. **Read phase analysis** - &#96;rebuild-roadmap/02-phases/01-phase-XX-*.md&#96;\n2. **Check changelog** - &#96;rebuild-roadmap/03-tracking/01-changelog.md&#96;\n3. **Use Context7** - query relevant documentation\n4. **Review docs** - understand requirements\n5. **Understand NOW state** - current implementation\n6. **Plan AFTER state** - target implementation\n7. **Check multi-platform strategy** - &#96;docs/03-platforms/01-multi-platform-strategy.md&#96;\n\n### During Implementation\n\n1. **Follow step-by-step** - don't skip steps\n2. **Implement on both platforms** - Mobile and Web simultaneously\n3. **Test frequently** - after each change on both platforms",

        "contents": "- [ ] Add all required fields (email, password, confirm password)\n- [ ] Add form validation\n- [ ] Add terms of service checkbox\n- [ ] Implement email verification flow\n- [ ] Add success message after signup\n- [ ] Connect to Supabase Auth\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Working signup screen\n\n**Success Criteria**: Can create account, receive verification email\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: Signup screen works, can create account, see success message\n\n---\n\n### Step 3.3: Implement Auth State Management\n**Goal**: Manage authentication state across the app\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create auth context/store (using Zustand or Context API)\n- [ ] Implement session persistence\n- [ ] Add auth state listeners (Supabase auth state changes)\n- [ ] Create protected route wrapper\n- [ ] Implement auto-logout on token expiry\n- [ ] Add refresh token logic\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Auth state management working\n\n**Success Criteria**: Auth state persists, protected routes work, auto-logout works\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: After login, stay logged in on app restart, protected screens accessible\n\n---\n\n### Step 3.4: Build Forgot Password Flow\n**Goal**: Password reset functionality\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create forgot password screen\n- [ ] Add email input and validation\n- [ ] Implement password reset email sending\n- [ ] Create reset password screen (for email link)\n- [ ] Add new password form with validation\n- [ ] Connect to Supabase Auth\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Working password reset flow\n\n**Success Criteria**: Can reset password via email\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: Can request reset, receive email, reset password\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4: Core Pages Structure\n\n### Step 4.1: Build Home/Sanctuary Screen\n**Goal**: Create main dashboard screen with empty state\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design home screen layout\n- [ ] Add header with user greeting\n- [ ] Add empty state for no content\n- [ ] Add quick action buttons (Create Affirmation, Create Meditation, Create Ritual)\n- [ ] Add navigation to other sections\n- [ ] Add placeholder for stats (will be populated later)\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Home screen with structure",

        "contents": "- [ ] Install and configure React Navigation\n- [ ] Create navigation types (TypeScript)\n- [ ] Set up stack navigator for auth flow\n- [ ] Set up tab navigator for main app\n- [ ] Create placeholder screens for all routes:\n  - Auth: Login, Signup, ForgotPassword\n  - Main: Home (Sanctuary), Library, Create, Profile\n  - Content: Affirmations, Meditations, Rituals\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Navigation structure in place\n- All routes accessible\n\n**Success Criteria**: Can navigate between all screens (empty screens)\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: Navigation bar visible, can tap between tabs, see empty screens\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2: Design System &amp; UI Foundation\n\n### Step 2.1: Create Design System\n**Goal**: Define colors, typography, spacing, and component styles\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Define color palette (primary, secondary, accent, background, text, error, success)\n- [ ] Define typography scale (headings, body, captions)\n- [ ] Define spacing system (4px, 8px, 16px, 24px, 32px, etc.)\n- [ ] Define border radius values\n- [ ] Define shadow/elevation system\n- [ ] Create theme configuration file\n- [ ] Set up dark mode support (if needed)\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- &#96;theme.ts&#96; or &#96;design-system.ts&#96; file\n- Color and typography constants\n\n**Success Criteria**: Theme system ready to use\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: No visible UI yet, but theme is defined\n\n---\n\n### Step 2.2: Build Core UI Components\n**Goal**: Create reusable UI components following design system\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Button component (primary, secondary, outline, text variants)\n- [ ] Input component (text, email, password, textarea)\n- [ ] Card component\n- [ ] Typography components (Heading, Body, Caption)\n- [ ] Loading/Spinner component\n- [ ] Icon component wrapper\n- [ ] Container/View wrapper components\n- [ ] Badge component\n- [ ] Progress indicator component\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Component library in &#96;components/ui/&#96;\n- All components documented with props\n\n**Success Criteria**: All components render correctly with theme\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: Component showcase screen showing all components\n\n---",

        "contents": "export default function LibraryPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const [contentType, setContentType] = useState&lt;ContentType&gt;('all');\n  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('');\n\n  // Placeholder data - replace with actual data from backend\n  const content: ContentItem[] = [\n    {\n      id: '1',\n      type: 'ritual',\n      title: 'Morning Empowerment',\n      description: 'Start your day with confidence and purpose',\n      frequency: '432Hz',\n      duration: '10:00',\n      lastPlayed: '2024-03-10',\n    },\n    {\n      id: '2',\n      type: 'affirmation',\n      title: 'Abundance Mindset',\n      description: 'Attract prosperity and success',\n      frequency: '528Hz',\n      duration: '5:00',\n      lastPlayed: '2024-03-12',\n    },\n    {\n      id: '3',\n      type: 'meditation',\n      title: 'Deep Relaxation',\n      description: 'Find peace and tranquility',\n      duration: '15:00',\n      lastPlayed: '2024-03-11',\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const filteredContent = content.filter((item) =&gt; {\n    if (contentType !== 'all' &amp;&amp; item.type !== contentType.slice(0, -1)) return false;\n    if (searchQuery &amp;&amp; !item.title.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())) return false;\n    return true;\n  });\n\n  const getTypeIcon = (type: string) =&gt; {\n    switch (type) {\n      case 'ritual':\n        return Music;\n      case 'affirmation':\n        return Sparkles;\n      case 'meditation':\n        return Brain;\n      default:\n        return LibraryIcon;\n    }\n  };\n\n  const getTypeColor = (type: string) =&gt; {\n    switch (type) {\n      case 'ritual':\n        return 'primary';\n      case 'affirmation':\n        return 'success';\n      case 'meditation':\n        return 'info';\n      default:\n        return 'default';\n    }\n  };\n\n  return (\n    &lt;Container&gt;\n      &lt;ThemeSelector /&gt;\n}",

        "contents": "# Phase 4: Core Pages Structure - Detailed Analysis\n\n## Overview\n**Goal**: Build the core page structure for Home, Library, Create, and Profile screens with empty states and navigation\n**Status**: ⏳ Pending → Ready to start after Phase 3\n**Dependencies**: Phase 1 (Project Foundation), Phase 2 (Design System), Phase 3 (Authentication)\n\n## Changelog\n\n### 2026-02-07 - Initial Analysis Created\n- **Status**: 📝 Analysis Complete\n- **Notes**: Created detailed phase analysis with NOW/AFTER comparison and implementation steps\n- **Next**: Begin after Phase 3 completion\n\n---\n\n## Current State Analysis (NOW)\n\n### Current Implementation\n**Location**: Placeholder screens from Phase 1\n\n**What Exists** (After Phases 1-3):\n- ✅ Navigation structure\n- ✅ Placeholder screens (empty)\n- ✅ Authentication working\n- ✅ Design system ready\n- ❌ No content structure\n- ❌ No empty states\n- ❌ No navigation between content\n\n**Current Schema**:\n- Database tables exist but not populated\n\n**Current Features**:\n- Navigation: ✅ Basic structure\n- Screens: ✅ Placeholders exist\n- Content: ❌ No content displayed\n- Empty States: ❌ Not designed\n\n---\n\n## Target State (AFTER)\n\n### Target Implementation\n**New Location**: &#96;src/screens/&#96; (Home, Library, Create, Profile)\n\n**What Will Exist**:\n- ✅ Home/Sanctuary screen with empty state\n- ✅ Library screen with filters and empty state\n- ✅ Create screen with three options\n- ✅ Profile/Settings screen\n- ✅ Beautiful empty states\n- ✅ Navigation between screens\n\n**Target Features**:\n- Home Screen: ✅ Dashboard with empty state\n- Library Screen: ✅ Filters, search placeholder, empty state\n- Create Screen: ✅ Three creation options\n- Profile Screen: ✅ User info, settings list\n\n---\n\n## Schema Verification\n\n### Current Schema (NOW)\n| Table/Field | Type | Status | Notes |\n|------------|------|--------|-------|\n| content_items | Table | ⏳ | May not exist yet |\n| profiles | Table | ✅ | Exists |\n\n### Target Schema (AFTER)\n| Table/Field | Type | Status | Notes |\n|------------|------|--------|-------|",

        "contents": "# Core Product Documentation Reference\n\n**Purpose**: Reference guide to main waQup product documentation\n\n**Location**: &#96;../../docs/internal/&#96; (main waQup project)\n\n---\n\n## How to Access\n\n**Direct Access**: &#96;../../docs/internal/&#96;  \n**Use Context7**: Query docs using Context7 (recommended)\n\n---\n\n## Complete Documentation List\n\n### Core Documents (01-07)\n\n1. **01-product-constitution.md**\n   - Core identity, principles, boundaries\n   - Three content types definition\n   - Voice-first design principles\n\n2. **02-scientific-foundations.md**\n   - Scientific backing and research\n   - Neuroplasticity, subconscious mind\n   - Voice prosody effects\n\n3. **03-conversational-system.md**\n   - Conversation flows\n   - Content types (affirmations, meditations, rituals)\n   - State machine\n\n4. **04-ai-voice-ethics.md**\n   - AI voice guidelines\n   - Ethical boundaries\n   - Privacy principles\n\n5. **05-system-architecture.md**\n   - Complete system architecture\n   - APIs, infrastructure\n   - Technology stack\n\n6. **06-value-economy.md**\n   - Credits system\n   - Tokens and economic model\n   - Pricing structure\n\n7. **07-roadmap.md**\n   - Product roadmap\n   - Milestones\n   - Development phases\n\n### Technical Reference\n\n- **database.md** - Database schema, tables, RLS policies\n- **flows.md** - User flows, ritual creation, affirmation flow\n- **system-spec.md** - Technical specifications\n- **system-audit.md** - Implementation status\n- **undocumented-features.md** - Implemented features catalog\n\n### Audio &amp; AI\n\n- **audio-generation.md** - Voice cloning, TTS, ElevenLabs\n- **elevenlabs-advantages.md** - Why ElevenLabs\n- **audio-generation-improvements.md** - Cost optimizations\n- **audio-generation-best-option-analysis.md** - TTS comparison\n- **audio-generation-tested-alternatives.md** - Tested alternatives\n\n### Platform &amp; Features\n\n- **marketplace-platform.md** - Marketplace features\n- **voice-interaction-design.md** - Voice interaction patterns\n\n---\n\n## Using Context7\n\n**Recommended**: Use Context7 to query these docs",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Core Product Documentation Reference",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "**Purpose**: Reference guide to main waQup product documentation",
            "lineNumber": 3

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./rebuild-roadmap/02-phases/01-phase-01-project-foundation.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 56
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Phase 1: Project Foundation - Detailed Analysis\n\n## Overview\n**Goal**: Set up clean, production-ready projects for Mobile (React Native + Expo for Android/iOS) and Web (Next.js for desktop browsers) simultaneously, configure all core dependencies, and establish connection to Supabase across both platforms\n**Status**: NOW → AFTER\n**Dependencies**: None (starting point)\n**Strategy**: Parallel development - Mobile and Web platforms set up simultaneously\n\n## Changelog\n\n### 2026-02-07 - Initial Analysis Created\n- **Status**: 📝 Analysis Complete\n- **Notes**: Created detailed phase analysis document with NOW/AFTER comparison, schema verification, and 5 implementation steps\n- **Next**: Begin Step 1.1 (Initialize React Native Expo Project)\n\n---\n\n## Current State Analysis (NOW)\n\n### Current Implementation\n**Location**: &#96;../../../&#96; (Next.js web app - parent waqup-app directory)\n\n**What Exists**:\n- ✅ Next.js 14 App Router application\n- ✅ Supabase integration (web)\n- ✅ TypeScript configuration\n- ✅ Tailwind CSS styling\n- ✅ Authentication system (web)\n- ✅ Database schema (PostgreSQL)\n- ✅ API routes (Next.js API routes)\n\n**Current Tech Stack**:\n- **Frontend**: Next.js 14, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS\n- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage)\n- **Deployment**: Vercel\n- **State Management**: React Context, Zustand (partial)\n- **Audio**: Web Audio API\n\n**Current Project Structure**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nwaqup-app/\n├── app/                    # Next.js App Router\n│   ├── (auth)/            # Auth routes\n│   ├── (sanctuary)/       # Protected routes\n│   ├── (onboarding)/      # Onboarding flow\n│   ├── (marketing)/       # Marketing pages\n│   ├── api/               # API routes\n│   ├── components/        # React components\n│   └── lib/               # Utilities\n├── docs/                  # Documentation\n├── public/                # Static assets\n├── supabase/              # Supabase config\n└── package.json           # Dependencies\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Current Limitations**:",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Phase 1: Project Foundation - Detailed Analysis",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "## Overview",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "**Goal**: Set up clean, production-ready projects for Mobile (React Native + Expo for Android/iOS) and Web (Next.js for desktop browsers) simultaneously, configure all core dependencies, and establish connection to Supabase across both platforms",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "**Status**: NOW → AFTER",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "**Dependencies**: None (starting point)",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "**Strategy**: Parallel development - Mobile and Web platforms set up simultaneously",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "## Changelog",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "### 2026-02-07 - Initial Analysis Created",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Status**: 📝 Analysis Complete",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Notes**: Created detailed phase analysis document with NOW/AFTER comparison, schema verification, and 5 implementation steps",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Next**: Begin Step 1.1 (Initialize React Native Expo Project)",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },

            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "**Location**: &#96;../../../&#96; (Next.js web app - parent waqup-app directory)",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "**What Exists**:",

            "text": "- ✅ Next.js 14 App Router application",

          {

          {
            "text": "- **Frontend**: Next.js 14, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS",

        "contents": "**UI Checkpoint**: Component showcase screen showing all components\n\n---\n\n### Step 2.3: Create Layout Components\n**Goal**: Build layout structure for screens\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Screen wrapper component (handles safe areas, padding)\n- [ ] Header component (with back button, title, actions)\n- [ ] Tab bar component (custom styling)\n- [ ] Bottom sheet component (for modals)\n- [ ] Drawer component (if needed)\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Layout components ready\n\n**Success Criteria**: Layout components work correctly\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See header and tab bar on all screens\n\n---\n\n### Step 2.4: Build Setup/Onboarding Page\n**Goal**: Create a beautiful setup page to verify design system\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Create welcome/setup screen\n- [ ] Use all design system elements\n- [ ] Add smooth animations (using react-native-reanimated)\n- [ ] Implement proper spacing and typography\n- [ ] Add illustrations or placeholder images\n- [ ] Make it responsive for different screen sizes\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Beautiful setup page\n\n**Success Criteria**: Page looks professional and uses design system correctly\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: See a polished setup/welcome page with all design elements\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3: Authentication System\n\n### Step 3.1: Build Login Screen\n**Goal**: Create functional login screen with validation\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design login screen UI\n- [ ] Add email and password inputs\n- [ ] Add form validation (email format, password requirements)\n- [ ] Add \"Forgot Password\" link\n- [ ] Add \"Sign Up\" link\n- [ ] Implement error message display\n- [ ] Add loading state during login\n- [ ] Connect to Supabase Auth\n\n**Deliverables**:\n- Working login screen\n\n**Success Criteria**: Can log in with valid credentials, see errors for invalid input\n\n**UI Checkpoint**: Beautiful login screen, can type, see validation errors, login works\n\n---\n\n### Step 3.2: Build Signup Screen\n**Goal**: Create signup flow with email verification\n\n**Tasks**:\n- [ ] Design signup screen UI\n- [ ] Add all required fields (email, password, confirm password)\n- [ ] Add form validation\n- [ ] Add terms of service checkbox",

        },
        "contents": "export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n\n [initializeAuth]);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Handle protected route redirects\n    if (!isReady || !isInitialized) return;\n\n    const publicRoutes = ['/', '/login', '/signup', '/forgot-password', '/reset-password', '/how-it-works', '/pricing'];\n    const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.includes(pathname) || pathname.startsWith('/showcase');\n    const isProtectedRoute = pathname.startsWith('/home') || \n                            pathname.startsWith('/library') || \n                            pathname.startsWith('/create') || \n                            pathname.startsWith('/profile');\n\n    if (isProtectedRoute &amp;&amp; !user) {\n      // Redirect to login if trying to access protected route without auth\n      router.push('/login');\n    } else if (!isPublicRoute &amp;&amp; !isProtectedRoute &amp;&amp; pathname !== '/') {\n      // If on an unknown route and not authenticated, redirect to home or login\n      if (!user) {\n        router.push('/login');\n      }\n    }\n  }, [user, isReady, isInitialized, pathname, router]);\n\n  // Show nothing while initializing\n  if (!isReady || !isInitialized) {\n    return null; // Or a loading screen component\n  }\n\n  return &lt;&gt;{children}&lt;/&gt;;\n}",

        "contents": "import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';\n\n/**\n * Proxy for protected routes\n * Checks authentication state and redirects if needed\n *\n * Note: This is a basic implementation. For production, you may want to\n * verify the session server-side or use a more robust approach.\n */\nexport function proxy(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  // Public routes that don't require authentication\n  const publicRoutes = ['/', '/login', '/signup', '/forgot-password', '/reset-password', '/how-it-works', '/pricing'];\n\n  // Check if route is public\n  const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.some(route =&gt; pathname === route || pathname.startsWith(route + '/'));\n\n  // Protected routes (main app routes)\n  const isProtectedRoute = pathname.startsWith('/home') ||\n                           pathname.startsWith('/library') ||\n                           pathname.startsWith('/create') ||\n                           pathname.startsWith('/profile');\n\n  // For protected routes, we'll handle redirect in the layout component\n  // since we need client-side auth state check\n  // Proxy can't access client-side state easily\n\n  return NextResponse.next();\n}\n\nexport const config = {\n  matcher: [\n    /*\n     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:\n     * - api (API routes)\n     * - _next/static (static files)\n     * - _next/image (image optimization files)\n     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)\n     * - public files (public folder)\n     */\n,\n};",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\nimport { useForm, Controller } from 'react-hook-form';\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';\nimport { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { Container, Typography, Button, Input } from '@/components';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/theme';\nimport { AnimatedBackground, Logo } from '@/components';\nimport { spacing, borderRadius } from '@/theme';\nimport { forgotPasswordSchema } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport { Mail, ArrowRight, CheckCircle2 } from 'lucide-react';\nimport type { ForgotPasswordFormData } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\n\nexport default function ForgotPasswordPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { requestPasswordReset, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const [emailSent, setEmailSent] = useState(false);\n  const [userEmail, setUserEmail] = useState('');\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;ForgotPasswordFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(forgotPasswordSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: ForgotPasswordFormData) =&gt; {\n    setError(null);\n    // For web, use the reset password page URL\n    const redirectTo = &#96;${window.location.origin}/reset-password&#96;;\n    const result = await requestPasswordReset(data.email, redirectTo);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      setUserEmail(data.email);\n      setEmailSent(true);\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  if (emailSent) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div\n}",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\nimport { useRouter, usePathname } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport { getOverrideUserToRestore } from '@/lib/auth-override';\n\n/**\n * Auth Provider Component\n * Initializes auth state and handles protected route redirects\n */\nexport function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n  const { user, isInitialized, initializeAuth } = useAuthStore();\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const pathname = usePathname();\n  const [isReady, setIsReady] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Initialize auth state on mount\n    let unsubscribe: (() =&gt; void) | null = null;\n    \n    initializeAuth().then((unsub) =&gt; {\n      unsubscribe = unsub;\n      // If no Supabase session but override is stored, restore override user so pages are visible\n      const overrideUser = getOverrideUserToRestore();\n      if (overrideUser) {\n        useAuthStore.getState().setUser(overrideUser);\n      }\n      setIsReady(true);\n    });\n    \n    return () =&gt; {\n      if (unsubscribe) {\n        unsubscribe();\n      }\n    };\n  }, [initializeAuth]);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Handle protected route redirects\n    if (!isReady || !isInitialized) return;\n  }\n}",

        "contents": "export default function ResetPasswordPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { resetPassword, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const authService = createAuthService(supabase);\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [showConfirmPassword, setShowConfirmPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [hasValidSession, setHasValidSession] = useState(false);\n  \n  // Check for Supabase session from hash fragments (handled automatically by Supabase)\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    const checkSession = async () =&gt; {\n      // Supabase automatically processes hash fragments and creates a session\n      // We just need to check if we have a session\n      const sessionResult = await authService.getCurrentSession();\n      setHasValidSession(!!sessionResult.data);\n    };\n    \n    checkSession();\n  }, [authService]);\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;ResetPasswordFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(resetPasswordSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      password: '',\n      confirmPassword: '',\n      token: '', // Token handled by Supabase from URL hash\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: ResetPasswordFormData) =&gt; {\n    if (!hasValidSession) {\n      setError('Invalid or expired reset link. Please request a new password reset.');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    setError(null);\n    // Supabase handles token from URL hash fragments automatically\n    const result = await resetPassword(data.password);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      router.push('/login?message=Password reset successful. Please sign in with your new password.');\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  if (!hasValidSession) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div\n}",

        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { useForm, Controller } from 'react-hook-form';\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';\nimport { useRouter, useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { Container, Typography, Button, Input } from '@/components';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/theme';\nimport { AnimatedBackground, Logo } from '@/components';\nimport { spacing, borderRadius } from '@/theme';\nimport { loginSchema } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport { applyOverrideLogin } from '@/lib/auth-override';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport { Mail, Lock, ArrowRight, Eye, EyeOff } from 'lucide-react';\nimport type { LoginFormData } from '@waqup/shared/schemas';\n\nexport default function LoginPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams();\n  const { login, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = React.useState(false);\n  const message = searchParams.get('message');\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;LoginFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(loginSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n      password: '',\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: LoginFormData) =&gt;\n;\n}",

        "contents": "export default function SignupPage() {\nconst \n;\n\n  if (signupSuccess) {\n    return (\n      &lt;div style={{ minHeight: '100vh', position: 'relative', overflow: 'hidden' }}&gt;\n        &lt;AnimatedBackground intensity=\"medium\" color=\"primary\" /&gt;\n        \n        &lt;div\n          style={{\n            position: 'fixed',\n            inset: 0,\n            background: colors.gradients.mystical,\n            pointerEvents: 'none',\n            zIndex: 0,\n          }}\n        /&gt;\n\n        &lt;div style={{ position: 'relative', zIndex: 1, minHeight: '100vh', display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center', padding: spacing.xl }}&gt;\n          &lt;div style={{ width: '100%', maxWidth: '480px' }}&gt;\n            &lt;div\n              style={{\n                padding: spacing.xxxl,\n                borderRadius: borderRadius.xl,\n                background: colors.glass.opaque,\n                backdropFilter: 'blur(20px)',\n                WebkitBackdropFilter: 'blur(20px)',\n                border: &#96;1px solid ${colors.glass.border}&#96;,\n                boxShadow: &#96;0 16px 64px ${colors.mystical.glow}40&#96;,\n                textAlign: 'center',\n              }}\n            &gt;\n              &lt;CheckCircle2 size={64} color={colors.success} style={{ margin: '0 auto', marginBottom: spacing.lg }} /&gt;\n              &lt;Typography variant=\"h2\" style={{ color: colors.text.primary, marginBottom: spacing.md }}&gt;\n                Check Your Email\n              &lt;/Typography&gt;\n              &lt;Typography variant=\"body\" style={{ color: colors.text.secondary, marginBottom: spacing.xl, lineHeight: '24px' }}&gt;\n                We've sent a verification email to &lt;strong&gt;{userEmail}&lt;/strong&gt;.         },
        "contents": "/**\n * Override login: validate credentials against env and allow bypassing Supabase.\n * Use only for dev/admin access. Set OVERRIDE_LOGIN_EMAIL and OVERRIDE_LOGIN_PASSWORD in env.\n */\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  try {\n    const body = await request.json();\n    const email = typeof body?.email === 'string' ? body.email.trim() : '';\n    const password = typeof body?.password === 'string' ? body.password : '';\n\n    const envEmail = process.env.OVERRIDE_LOGIN_EMAIL?.trim();\n    const envPassword = process.env.OVERRIDE_LOGIN_PASSWORD;\n\n    if (!envEmail || !envPassword) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: 'Override login not configured' },\n        { status: 501 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (email === envEmail &amp;&amp; password === envPassword) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    }\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: 'Invalid credentials' },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: 'Bad request' },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n}",

{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Programmatic Sitemap Generation with TypeScript\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/01-metadata/sitemap.mdx\\n\\nGenerate a sitemap dynamically using a sitemap.ts file that exports a function returning an array of URL objects. This approach allows dynamic URL generation and is cached by default unless using Dynamic APIs.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;APIDOC\\n## POST app/sitemap.ts\\n\\n### Description\\nExport a default function from &#96;sitemap.ts&#96; that returns an array of sitemap URL objects. Next.js automatically generates and caches the XML sitemap from this function.\\n\\n### File Location\\n&#96;app/sitemap.ts&#96;\\n\\n### Method\\nGET (automatic Route Handler)\\n\\n### Returns\\nArray of &#96;MetadataRoute.Sitemap&#96; objects\\n\\n### Return Type Structure\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\ninterface SitemapEntry {\\n  url: string\\n  lastModified?: Date | string\\n  changeFrequency?: 'yearly' | 'monthly' | 'weekly' | 'daily' | 'hourly' | 'never'\\n  priority?: number\\n  images?: string[]\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Request Example\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\nimport type { MetadataRoute } from 'next'\\n\\nexport default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap {\\n  return [\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'yearly',\\n      priority: 1,\\n    },\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com/about',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'monthly',\\n      priority: 0.8,\\n    },\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com/blog',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'weekly',\\n      priority: 0.5,\\n    },\\n  ]\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Response\\n#### Success Response (200 - Generated XML)\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;xml\\n&lt;urlset xmlns=\\\"http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9\\\"&gt;\\n  &lt;url&gt;\\n    &lt;loc&gt;https://acme.com&lt;/loc&gt;\\n    &lt;lastmod&gt;2023-04-06T15:02:24.021Z&lt;/lastmod&gt;\\n    &lt;changefreq&gt;yearly&lt;/changefreq&gt;\\n    &lt;priority&gt;1&lt;/priority&gt;\\n  &lt;/url&gt;\\n  &lt;url&gt;\\n    &lt;loc&gt;https://acme.com/about&lt;/loc&gt;\\n    &lt;lastmod&gt;2023-04-06T15:02:24.021Z&lt;/lastmod&gt;\\n    &lt;changefreq&gt;monthly&lt;/changefreq&gt;\\n    &lt;priority&gt;0.8&lt;/priority&gt;\\n  &lt;/url&gt;\\n  &lt;url&gt;\\n    &lt;loc&gt;https://acme.com/blog&lt;/loc&gt;\\n    &lt;lastmod&gt;2023-04-06T15:02:24.021Z&lt;/lastmod&gt;\\n    &lt;changefreq&gt;weekly&lt;/changefreq&gt;\\n    &lt;priority&gt;0.5&lt;/priority&gt;\\n  &lt;/url&gt;\\n&lt;/urlset&gt;\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Configuration\\n- **Caching**: Cached by default unless using Dynamic APIs or dynamic config options\\n- **Content-Type**: Automatically set to &#96;application/xml&#96;\\n- **Path**: Accessible at &#96;/sitemap.xml&#96;\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Generate a dynamic sitemap in Next.js using JavaScript or TypeScript\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/01-metadata/sitemap.mdx\\n\\nThis code demonstrates how to programmatically generate a sitemap by exporting a default function that returns an array of URL objects. Each object specifies the URL, last modification date, change frequency, and priority. This approach is suitable for larger applications or dynamic content, leveraging Next.js's &#96;MetadataRoute.Sitemap&#96; type for TypeScript users.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\nimport type { MetadataRoute } from 'next'\\n\\nexport default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap {\\n  return [\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'yearly',\\n      priority: 1,\\n    },\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com/about',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'monthly',\\n      priority: 0.8,\\n    },\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com/blog',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'weekly',\\n      priority: 0.5,\\n    },\\n  ]\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\\nexport default function sitemap() {\\n  return [\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'yearly',\\n      priority: 1,\\n    },\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com/about',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'monthly',\\n      priority: 0.8,\\n    },\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com/blog',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      changeFrequency: 'weekly',\\n      priority: 0.5,\\n    },\\n  ]\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Define Localized Sitemaps in Next.js\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/01-metadata/sitemap.mdx\\n\\nThis snippet demonstrates how to configure localized sitemaps in Next.js for internationalized applications, supporting multiple languages. It uses the &#96;alternates.languages&#96; property within the &#96;MetadataRoute.Sitemap&#96; definition to specify &#96;hreflang&#96; attributes, ensuring search engines can correctly identify and serve language-specific versions of pages. Examples are provided for TypeScript and JavaScript, alongside the resulting XML output.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\nimport type { MetadataRoute } from 'next'\\n\\nexport default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap {\\n  return [\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://acme.com',\\n      lastModified: new Date(),\\n      alternates: {\\n        languages: {\\n          es: information using the &#96;images&#96; property within a URL object. It exports a default function that returns an array of sitemap entries, each specifying a URL and an array of associated image URLs. This helps search engines discover images on the site.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\nimport type { MetadataRoute } from 'next'\\n\\nexport default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap {\\n  return [\\n    {\\n      url: 'https://example.com',\\n      lastModified: '2021-01-01',\\n      changeFrequency: 'weekly',\\n      priority: 0.5,\\n      images: ['https://example.com/image.jpg'],\\n    },\\n  ]\\n}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### sitemap.xml &gt; Generating a sitemap using code (.js, .ts)\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/01-metadata/sitemap.mdx\\n\\nYou can use the &#96;sitemap.(js|ts)&#96; file convention to programmatically **generate** a sitemap by exporting a default function that returns an array of URLs. If using TypeScript, a [&#96;Sitemap&#96;](#returns) type is available. &#96;sitemap.js&#96; is a special Route Handler that is cached by default unless it uses a [Dynamic API](/docs/app/guides/caching#dynamic-apis) or [dynamic config](/docs/app/guides/caching#segment-config-options) option.\"}]}"

Summary of what was implemented:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Redundancy fixes
- **Removed duplicate `ThemeSelector`** from (main) pages: home, library, create, profile (they use the layout)
- **Removed redundant `ThemeProvider`** from (main), (auth), and (marketing) layouts (root layout only)
- **Updated create page links** to sanctuary paths: `/sanctuary/affirmations/create`, `/sanctuary/meditations/create`, `/sanctuary/rituals/create`

### 2. New pages
- **`/pages`** – index of all routes by category
- **`/sitemap`** – HTML sitemap
- **`app/sitemap.ts`** – XML sitemap for SEO

### 3. Auth
- **`/confirm-email`** – email verification (in `(auth)` layout)
- **`/auth/beta-signup`** – beta signup with layout

### 4. Onboarding
- **`(onboarding)` layout** with `ThemeSelector`
- **`/onboarding`**, **`/onboarding/profile`**, **`/onboarding/preferences`**, **`/onboarding/guide`**

### 5. Sanctuary
- **`sanctuary` layout** with `ThemeSelector`
- **Sanctuary:** `/sanctuary`, settings, credits, progress, referral, reminders, learn
- **Affirmations:** list, create (steps), record
- **Rituals:** list, `[id]`, `[id]/edit`, create, recordings
- **Meditations:** create (for create page link)

### 6. Link updates
- **Home:** Quick actions and menu items point to `/sanctuary/reminders`, `/sanctuary/learn`, `/sanctuary/progress`, `/sanctuary/credits`, `/sanctuary/settings`
- **Profile:** Menu items point to sanctuary routes

### 7. Shared component
- **`PlaceholderPage`** – shared component used for new placeholder pages

### 8. Auth & navigation updates
- **AuthProvider** – public and protected routes updated
- **proxy.ts** – public and protected routes updated
- **`navigation.ts`** – route types and constants extended

All new pages use the theme popup (`ThemeSelector`) via their layout. No duplicate or redundant routes or layouts were added.

---
